summary: Organized crime group used stolen credentials obtained via phishing to access a banking application database. 15,000 customer records were exfiltrated before detection.
A hospital employee sent patient appointment reminders to the wrong email distribution list, exposing 80 patient names and appointment details.
Threat actor sent targeted spear-phishing emails to finance staff at an insurance company, harvesting credentials used to access web-based accounting systems.
Nation-state actor exploited a VPN vulnerability to gain persistent access to a government ministry, deploying custom backdoor malware for long-term espionage.
A medical practice received a malicious email attachment disguised as an invoice, deploying ransomware that encrypted patient records and administrative files.
A database administrator at a retail chain abused privileged access to extract and sell 2,200 payment card records to an underground marketplace.
Business email compromise attack against a small accounting firm; attacker impersonated a CFO to redirect a wire transfer of €45,000.
Hacktivists brute-forced weak admin credentials on a municipal government website and defaced the homepage with political messaging.
A hotel chain's third-party booking platform vendor misconfigured a cloud storage bucket, exposing 42,000 customer reservation records publicly for three weeks.
SQL injection attack against an e-commerce site's product search function yielded access to the customer database, including partial payment card data.
Phishing campaign targeted employees of an accounting firm, compromising email accounts and gaining access to client financial data stored in shared drives.
A university administrator accidentally published a student registration spreadsheet containing personal details on a publicly accessible web server.
Hospital network infected with ransomware through a drive-by download on a clinical workstation, encrypting patient records and causing a 12-hour operational outage.
State-sponsored actor maintained covert access to a defence electronics manufacturer for two months, systematically exfiltrating R&D documents.
DDoS attack against a regional media company's streaming service caused a 4-hour outage during a major live broadcast event.
An insurance agent leaving for a competitor emailed themselves a spreadsheet of 600 client records before resigning, violating data handling policies.
A phishing email with a malicious macro-enabled document installed a banking trojan on an e-commerce operator's system, capturing payment card data processed by the business.
A SaaS provider misconfigured access controls on a production database, leaving customer account data including hashed passwords accessible without authentication for 48 hours.
Ransomware group exploited an unpatched vulnerability in a medical practice's patient portal to gain entry, ultimately encrypting EHR systems and demanding €180,000.
A civil servant left an unencrypted laptop containing constituent case files on public transport; the device was not recovered.
Smishing and vishing campaign targeted retail banking customers, convincing victims to reveal OTP codes, leading to unauthorised account transfers totalling €23,000.
Supply-chain compromise via a malicious software update from a trusted vendor allowed state-sponsored actors to gain persistent access to an aerospace company's R&D environment.
Attackers used credential-stuffing against a dental practice's remote desktop service, gaining access to patient records and scheduling systems.
Developer accidentally set a cloud storage bucket containing source code and environment configuration files to public, exposing API keys and internal documentation.
Retail employees clicked a fake order-confirmation phishing email, compromising three sets of internal system credentials used for inventory management.
Nation-state actors maintained stealthy access to a ministry of foreign affairs network for three months using a rootkit, exfiltrating diplomatic communications.
Ransomware was deployed via a compromised remote desktop session at a small construction firm, encrypting all shared drives and demanding €25,000 in cryptocurrency.
A successful phishing attack against a health insurance company compromised an employee email account, exposing customer personal and health data stored in email attachments.
A recently terminated system administrator used retained privileged credentials to delete critical database backups and configuration files at a software company, causing significant recovery costs.
Web skimmer injected into an e-commerce checkout page via a vulnerable third-party JavaScript library, silently harvesting 3,000 payment card numbers over four days.
A legal firm suffered a targeted phishing attack, installing a remote access tool on a partner's laptop and allowing the attacker to access confidential client documents.
A cloud services vendor deployed a misconfigured database instance for a university, exposing 25,000 student and staff records to the public internet.
Phishing campaign impersonating a bank's IT department harvested credentials from eight employees, enabling access to internal banking systems.
A small retail chain had all point-of-sale and back-office systems encrypted by ransomware introduced through a brute-forced RDP endpoint, causing a 3-day operational shutdown.
A medical receptionist sent lab results to the wrong patient via email due to an autocomplete error, exposing personal health information for 45 individuals.
An online retailer's e-commerce platform was compromised through a deserialization vulnerability, resulting in long-term exfiltration of customer personal and payment card data.
A volumetric DDoS attack from a botnet took down a small online shop's website for 10 hours, causing significant revenue loss during a promotional sale period.
Phishing attack compromised an employee's Microsoft 365 credentials at a consulting firm, enabling access to client project files and sensitive correspondence stored in SharePoint.
A hospital IT administrator systematically accessed and sold patient records to a private health data broker over a 20-day period before being caught by a data loss prevention alert.
Local government authority hit by double-extortion ransomware via a compromised VPN gateway; attackers exfiltrated citizen data before encrypting systems and demanding €500,000.
Construction company employees fell for a fake document-signing phishing page, compromising credentials to the company's project management and invoicing platforms.
A developer misconfigured database permissions during a deployment, temporarily exposing 750 user records including email addresses and password hashes.
A travel agency's booking platform was compromised through stolen admin credentials, enabling installation of a backdoor and exfiltration of customer booking and passport data.
BEC fraud via a combination of phishing and social engineering phone calls resulted in an unauthorised bank transfer of €78,000 from a small trading company.
A ransomware attachment sent to school administrative staff encrypted all shared administrative systems, disrupting operations for two days and requiring a full restore from backup.
An IT vendor performing maintenance on an insurance company's systems accidentally copied a production customer database to a shared, publicly accessible test environment.
A healthcare software vendor's patient portal was exploited via an SQL injection vulnerability in the search function, exposing 14,000 patient personal records and 3,000 diagnostic results.
A small electronics manufacturer's CEO email account was compromised through an OAuth phishing attack, exposing confidential supplier contracts and negotiation documents.
State-sponsored threat actors exploited an internet-facing research portal to gain access to a national scientific institute, conducting intelligence collection over 25 days before discovery.
A bank employee sent a bulk email containing customer account balance information to the wrong recipient list, exposing personal financial data for 130 customers.
